content:
Member-only story

Most Angular Developers Meet the Facade Pattern Too Late

3 min read
4 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The first time many developers hear about the Facade Pattern, the reaction is usually the same.
“Why create another class that just forwards method calls?”
It looks like unnecessary boilerplate.
Another file.
Another layer.
Another abstraction.
At first glance…
The criticism feels reasonable.
Until your application starts growing.
Components Slowly Become Architects
Imagine a simple component.
CustomerComponentInitially it:

• Loads customers

• Displays data

• Handles button clicks
Nothing unusual.
Then new requirements arrive.
The component now knows about:

• API calls

• State management

• Permissions

• Analytics
